rule.name: Kubernetes Create or Update Privileged Pod
rule.description: The following analytic detects the creation or update of privileged pods in Kubernetes. It identifies this activity by monitoring Kubernetes Audit logs for pod configurations that include root privileges. This behavior is significant for a SOC as it could indicate an attempt to escalate privileges, exploit the kernel, and gain full access to the host's namespace and devices. If confirmed malicious, this activity could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information, data breaches, and service disruptions, posing a severe threat to the environment.
rule.query:
`kube_audit` objectRef.resource=pods verb=create OR verb=update requestObject.metadata.annotations.kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration=*\"privileged\":true*
  | fillnull
  | stats count values(user.groups{}) as user_groups
    BY kind objectRef.name objectRef.namespace
       objectRef.resource requestObject.kind responseStatus.code
       sourceIPs{} stage user.username
       userAgent verb requestObject.metadata.annotations.kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration
  | rename sourceIPs{} as src_ip, user.username as user
  | `kubernetes_create_or_update_privileged_pod_filter`
false_positives: No false positives have been identified at this time.
